operating_state: Revenue reached $5.66 billion, up 84% year-over-year and 13% sequentially, driven by broad growth across all platforms. Gaming revenue increased 106% year-over-year from GeForce RTX 30 Series sales and cryptocurrency mining demand. Data Center revenue grew 79% year-over-year driven by Mellanox acquisition and NVIDIA Ampere architecture ramp into hyperscale customers.
cost_structure: Gross margin decreased 100 basis points year-over-year to 64.1% due to Mellanox acquisition-related intangible asset amortization and Data Center product mix shift. Operating expenses increased 63% year-over-year driven by Mellanox acquisition and employee growth. Tax rate outlook increased to 13% plus or minus 1% from prior 10% guidance, with one-time discrete tax benefit of $250-300 million expected from domestication transaction.
material_events: Board declared four-for-one stock split pending stockholder approval to increase authorized shares from 2 billion to 4 billion. Company completed domestication of foreign subsidiary on June 28, 2021, repatriating economic rights of non-U.S. intellectual property to the United States. Launched CMP cryptocurrency mining products generating $155 million revenue. Issued new debt securities across four tranches maturing 2023-2031.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues creating supply chain constraints and component availability limitations, particularly in Gaming segment, potentially requiring excess inventory orders or premium payments. Cryptocurrency market volatility impacts GPU demand estimation, with pending Ethereum 2.0 standard potentially increasing aftermarket GPU resales. Corporate structure realignment responds to global tax law changes.
forward_outlook: Management expects to remain supply-constrained into second half of fiscal year, primarily in Gaming segment, with potential need to place non-cancellable inventory orders significantly in advance. Arm acquisition closure anticipated in first quarter of calendar 2022 subject to regulatory approvals. Updated tax rate guidance reflects permanent structural change from intellectual property repatriation.
uncertainty_profile: Pending Arm acquisition faces regulatory review across multiple jurisdictions with $1.25 billion refund provision if terminated under certain circumstances. COVID-19 pandemic continues creating demand estimation challenges and supply chain disruptions. Cryptocurrency mining demand remains difficult to quantify and subject to rapid changes from market volatility and technical standard modifications.
